sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution accepting a $40,000 grant from the National Endowment for the Arts
(NEA). The grant will be used to support a Civic Arts grant program totaling $80,000 to
be expended in Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26). This will be the second annual implementation
of the new program designed to support the following types of arts and cultural
programming in the City of Berkeley:
 Nonprofit (not-art specific) organizations located in the City of Berkeley to
produce arts/cultural programming in the City of Berkeley led by a partnering arts
organization or teaching artist; or
 Arts nonprofit organizations located in the Greater Bay Area (outside of Berkeley)
to produce arts/cultural programming taking place in the City of Berkeley.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The NEA requires a one-to-one match for this grant award of $40,000, which would be
funded by the General Fund (Fund 011) in FY26 resulting in a total of $80,000 to be
expended in FY26 as the second annual implementation of the Arts Programs Grant
category administered by civic arts staff in the Office of Economic Development.
sections.current_situation:
In November 2024, the City of Berkeley was awarded a $40,000 grant from the NEA to
to continue implementation for a second year of the new Civic Arts grant program (the
Arts Programs Grant) totaling $80,000 in Fiscal Year 2026. The Arts Programs Grant
category aims to (1) foster cross-sector collaborations and opportunities for non-arts
organizations to hire arts and cultural workers or partner with Berkeley-based arts
organizations for programming, and (2) offer the Berkeley community the opportunity to
engage in Greater Bay Area programming offered by non-Berkeley arts organizations
taking place in Berkeley. Grant Awards will be up to $5,000 each for eligible program-
related expenses. The review criteria for the new grant category are: Program Narrative

sections.background:
Civic Arts staff initially applied for funding from the NEA in 2023 in order to establish a
new “Arts Programs Grant” category as a pilot for FY25 to address an identified gap in
the Civic Arts Grants offerings and better support a wide range of arts and cultural
activities taking place in Berkeley. With an annual budget of approximately $700,000
from the City's General Fund (011), the Civic Arts Grants Program has historically
awarded funding in three distinct categories: 1) Individual Artist Projects; 2) Community
Festivals, and 3) General Operating Support. This NEA grant funding allows for the
continuation of a fourth Civic Arts Grants funding category – the Arts Programs Grant,
which will be administered in conjunction with the FY26 Civic Arts Grants process. This
is the second year the City of Berkeley has been awarded a grant from the National
Endowment for the Arts for sub-granting purposes. Authorization for the NEA grant
application in accordance with the City’s administrative regulations was approved by
Council on February 13, 2024 (Resolution 71,208–N.S.). After the success of the first
year’s Arts Programs Grant pilot program, the NEA increased the award amount by
$10,000 (from $30,000 for FY25 to $40,000 for FY26).
The Arts Programs Grant category allowed Civic Arts to engage twelve new grantees in
FY25 with the first year’s NEA award.1This federal grant enables the City of Berkeley to
leverage local funds to provide increased support for arts and cultural activities to
benefit Berkeley residents. The current NEA grant award, plus the required City match,
represents a total increase over the historical Civic Arts Grants budget from $700,000 to
$780,00 (approximately an 11.5% increase).
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Accepting federal funding from the National Endowment for the Arts would help the City
accomplish one of the five high-level goals articulated in The City of Berkeley’s Arts and
Culture Plan (2018-2027): “Increase Investment in a Vibrant Arts Community,
Strengthen and support Berkeley’s inclusive and culturally rich arts sector by expanding
funding and marketing opportunities for a broader array of arts organizations.”
Additionally, accepting these funds aligns with the City of Berkeley’s fiscal policy to
leverage City funds to secure matching State and Federal resources for the City of
Berkeley.
1 A complete list of City of Berkeley Civic Arts grantees is available online: https://berkeleyca.gov/community-
recreation/civic-arts/civic-arts-grants and https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/documents/Civic-Arts-Grants-
FY24.pdf.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###N.S.
ACCEPTING A $40,000 GRANT FROM THE NATIONAL ENDOWMENT FOR THE
ARTS (NEA) TO SUPPORT ACCESS TO ARTS AND CULTURAL PROGRAMMING IN
THE CITY OF BERKELEY BY CONTINUING THE NEW CIVC ARTS GRANTS
PROGRAM-BASED FUNDING CATEGORY KNOWN AS THE ARTS PROGRAMS
GRANT.
WHEREAS, the Arts and Culture Plan for the City of Berkeley (2017-18) includes the goal
of increasing investment in a vibrant arts community by expanding funding and marketing
opportunities for a broader array of arts organizations; and
WHEREAS, the Civic Arts Program has identified a gap in its current grant program
offerings through data analysis, and community feedback on program-based funding; and
WHEREAS, an Arts Programs Grant would support the City’s ability to create increased
access to arts and cultural programming taking place in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, federal funding from the National Endowment for the Arts is available to local
arts agencies for sub-granting purposes; and
WHEREAS, the City has identified $40,000 in matching funds in FY26 to use for a new
grant program category, the Arts Programs Grant of $80,000 total; and
WHEREAS, the National Endowment for the Arts has awarded a $40,000 grant for this
purpose and these funds will be put into revenue account code 336-21-208-252-0000-
000-000-434110.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
$40,000 grant from the National Endowment for the Arts be accepted to support access
to arts and cultural programing in the City of Berkeley by continuing the new civic arts
programs grants.
